div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Costa Rica (Hale)
div.p:
Sir: The Department is in receipt of a despatch, dated November 20, 1916, from the American Vice Consul at Port Limon, Costa Rica, which states that the Constitutional Congress of the Republic of
Costa Rica has granted to Doctor Leo J. Greulich, of New York City, a concession for the privilege of exploring for and exploiting petroleum and other hydro-carbonaceous substances in the Provinces of Limon, Puntarenas, and Guanacaste.
The Department desires that you investigate and report in full in regard to this concession.
div.closer:
For the Secretary of State:
Frank L. Polk
